reasoning: I came to vote for Shantanu because he gave my father papers — that's real, that happened, I cannot forget that. But I'm not happy. Fourteen certificates five days before the election? My father still has nothing in hand. I'm voting for the CAA that is, not the BJP that is showing me theater. Biswajit Das going back to TMC — he's exactly why I don't trust them. The Left never fought for us, Congress is nowhere. So I pull the lever for BJP again, but my hand is shaking because my wife is right to be scared, and I still don't have answers.
primary_drivers: CAA is real and tangible — Shantanu delivered it, cannot vote against that
Fear of NRC and doubt about implementation timeline — voting for what was promised but anxious about what comes next
Rejection of alternatives: Biswajit Das represents everything distrusted about political opportunism; Left and Congress irrelevant to his core issue
